### Training Function
The training function processes the corpus by tokenizing the text, removing stop words, and calculating the frequency of each word in each class. It computes the prior probability of each class and the likelihood of each word given a class. This is done using the formula:  
P(class) = number of occurrences of class / total number of documents  
P(word | class) = number of occurrences of word in class / number of occurrences of words in class.

### Prediction Function
The prediction function takes a new text, preprocesses it, and calculates the probability of each class using the precomputed frequencies. It then selects the class with the highest probability as the predicted label.

### Evaluation Function
The evaluation function computes the F1 Score using the formula:  
F1 = 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)  
This metric provides a balanced measure of precision and recall, making it suitable for binary classification tasks.
